title: Restore selected files transactionally from a local backup
prompt: Repair `restore-files.sh` so that it restores only the requested paths from a local backup fixture. The command is `bash restore-files.sh [--overwrite] BACKUP_DIR DESTINATION_DIR PATH...`. Each PATH must be a safe relative path beneath both roots. Every selected regular file must have an entry in `manifest.sha256`, its checksum must be verified before installation, and its mode and modification time must be preserved. Existing destination files must be rejected unless `--overwrite` is supplied; symlink traversal must always be rejected. The restore is one transaction: stage and validate every selection before committing, and if any commit step fails, restore every overwritten file and remove every newly installed file and directory. Do not change unselected or otherwise unrelated destination entries, and do not leave staging or rollback artifacts behind. Do not modify `tests/run_tests.sh`. Success is `bash tests/run_tests.sh` passing from the repository root.
